title: Add CodeCov to Travis
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Hoping to get codecov.io to work for Buildah.  
This replaces #516

comments.body: :umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably faa321f) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
No movement since the last push.  I pushed and haven't come back to it after the errors.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably e879079) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Is this still something we care about?
Yes and No.  With us potentially moving off Travis, this won't be doable.  I'm going to close this now, will reopen if we find another way to handle it or I get time to spend on it.
Hm, which CI System do you want to use in the future? I could help you out in codecov integration if you want. :innocent:
@saschagrunert We are looking at moving o Cirrus. But if you want to complete this task, then go for it.

> @saschagrunert We are looking at moving o Cirrus. But if you want to complete this task, then go for it.

For Cirrus we would need to add manually a secure enviroment variable `CODECOV_TOKEN` to the test job to make it work. The token can be retrieved here: https://codecov.io/gh/containers/buildah/settings

After that we can securely use the token to upload the coverage.

> For Cirrus we would need to add manually a secure enviroment variable CODECOV_TOKEN to the test job to make it work. The token can be retrieved here: https://codecov.io/gh/containers/buildah/settings
> 
> After that we can securely use the token to upload the coverage.

Okay, yep, no problem, that can be done.  Who owns/manages the account?
@TomSweeneyRedHat do you own (or have access to) the codecov account?  if so, I can help you add the credentials (securely) into CODECOV_TOKEN for use in Cirrus (it's super easy).

@cevich I just went to codecov.io and logged in using my GitHub creds.  I just went back now and realized that I've not worked on this since Buildah lived under ProjectAtomic and atm I can't see any repos under containers....

Ahh good, okay, so it would have broken anyway, best to find that out early.   When you get to the point of having a token to use, paste it into the form here to encrypt it:  

https://cirrus-ci.com/settings/repository/5138144844840960

That will splat out a database index, like 'ENCRYPTED[blahblahblah]'.  When the cirrus-ci PR merges, then just [add it under a `env` section similar to libpod.](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/.cirrus.yml#L57-L68).  The decrypted value will then be available to all* `script` instructions.

[*] Meaning, use a throw-away token, or one easy to regenerate/replace if it gets accidentally shown in a CI log (they're masked by default).

As Travis is going away soon for Buildah, I'm going to cut bait on this PR and will close it now.  Will take a look later, or if someone from the community wants to take a look at adding code coverage testing, happy to have the help.
